facts.text: Ilion is a village in Herkimer County, NY, located on the south bank of the Mohawk River.
The village was incorporated in 1852 and remains one of only twelve New York villages still governed by its original charter.
Eliphalet Remington founded the first rifle factory in Ilion around 1816, launching the Remington Arms company that would become a global firearms brand.
The completion of the Erie Canal in 1825 linked Ilion to the Great Lakes, boosting trade and spurring rapid growth.
Ilion’s First United Methodist Church, Thomas Richardson House, Remington Stables, and U.S. Post Office are listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
In 2024, the 1‑million‑square‑foot Remington Arms factory closed, ending a century‑long manufacturing presence in the village.
